speaker: MATT
raw_text: Too late. So as you're looking around, you see this strange quiver of shadow that seems to formulate in the air, and this awkward, still, unmoving, awkward mannequin-type visual recreation of Vax muddles itself there. I need you to roll an insight check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You go ahead and explain.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: No, go right ahead.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is a fine, smooth, dark metal warhammer that as the handle itself leads towards the heavy head portion of the hammer, the metal turns instead to almost a glistening ivory that has symbols pushed into it. A language you don't understand, but there's a giant moon crescent carved into each side of the hammer's head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Whoa.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: It took me weeks to forge it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: This is beautiful. Can I hit something with it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What can I hit with it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan, where are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan, come here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What's up? What do you need?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Just stand right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Whatever you need.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I reach back halfway and give a quarter-measure (whoomp). Right into Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an attack roll. We'll say with disadvantage because you were taking a half-measure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: About the same as the Firebrand-ish?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's technically non-magical.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 14. You manage to just barely, as you see this thing coming towards you, and duck beneath it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I thought you were going to tell me a dirty joke, or something! When you say "come here," usually that's what that means!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, I wanted to try out the hammer.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Well then, no! Try it on Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm already 20 paces away.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Vax?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I go stealth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll stealth. Roll a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Vax! Shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Two. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Try as you might to look around, Vax is nowhere to be seen.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I literally just pivoted around my sister and pressed against her.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Doing exactly what she does?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to use Minor Illusion to make a really shitty Vax. A really cruddy-looking Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not in Vasselheim!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit. Too late.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I was going to say, based on the push of the water against the rock and Bigby's Hand against that, I need you to go ahead and roll a d20 and add eight for Bigby's strength to try and hold the plug in place.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, can you walk down this way while still doing that, and Scanlan, can you use Bigby's Hand to block the hole once Keyleth is past you?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: For a very small amount of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, and then Keyleth, can you make a wall of stone once you've let down the water?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, if we try and go super fast.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I can cast Light and get us down there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I can make a wall behind us or something.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I go and start heading towards the tunnel and pushing all the water outwards.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so keeping the water at bay, now rotating around your movement into the center, you all make your way towards that rocky entrance. It's just big enough to get each of you through, Grog and Trinket, at the very largest. You all push your way through, climbing in the other side. Keyleth, concentrating, steps inside as the last one as you begin, with the last few minutes you have.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I guess I'll just pack it with mud and rocks, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Just taking it all. Chunk after chunk after chunk. Just at the tail end of your concentration. The hand pulls in the last plug as the water (crashing water sound) comes crashing into itself, filling the space. As you all are standing there, the light of Zahra's staff lighting the area, there's the impact of water rushing in against the stonework, and you can feel the whole place shake from the impact, and immediately water begins to rush in through the cracks of this created section.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm not a good builder! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hold it, Scanlan, hold it for a moment!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you step forward and begin to try, pulling your tools out to inspect the nature of these, looking into the dark paths on each side, there's an instantaneous movement, like a puff of air that comes out of each side. I need you to roll a dexterity save.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, was I supposed to still be at disadvantage?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If she's behind you with a very faint bit of candlelight. You guys have low-light vision, so you can still make these checks right now, but it's at disadvantage because it's very, very limited.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: The twins, baby.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. As you guys push forward, you reach out and stop Vax before he takes another step as you notice this pathway now, hatchbacking back in the direction you guys had come from on the lower floor. You can see these very, very thin, about two inch wide slits in the stone walls to each side of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to take a closer look at those things. Do I need to roll anything if I see them now, because she's pointed them out?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She pointed them out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to try to disarm them. They look like things I've seen before. They look like traps. I'm going to try to disarm them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I extend my staff and I raise the light just a bit so you can see.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does it look like traps? You want to perceive them?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What is disarm traps? Dexterity check?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This would be using your thieves' tools, and for this it would be-- I will make this an intellect check, with the tools.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: An intellect check? Not the thieves' tools?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Thieves' tools, proficiency, plus your intelligence modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Plus the intelligence modifier?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct, because this is-- you actually have not encountered this type of trap before, and you're trying to ascertain its nature without setting it off.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I need popcorn for this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 23, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wow, you're really smart.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That's the end of your turn, that's all your attacks. Okay. That ends it with Percy. At the end of your turn, Percy, it's going to do one of its eye rays. This one is going to be towards Grog, because Grog is out of the cone and rushed underneath, throwing the blade up, and stuck into it. One of the eyestalks shoots down-- Okay, Grog, I need you to roll a dexterity saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Say 'screw it.'

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Totally.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So you can just chime in and say, 'Fuck what I said, I'm going now'?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So, my other question is, my attempt to cast a spell while in the cone-- does that cancel out that spell so I can't cast it anymore?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, the spell is used, unfortunately. Beholders, man. They're assholes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to make a run for cover towards the-- where's the laser pointer?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Towards which way?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Run that way. There. Am I still in its--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: One more, if you want to.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, and while I'm running I'm going to take a shot, I'm going to pull out the big gun and take a shot with the big gun.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And I'm going to attempt to make this a legs/wing hit, and I'm going to try and knock him down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Does not have legs, does not have wings.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit, okay, that's fair.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hovering entities aren't really affected by prone. You would know that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No they're not. That's very fair. In that case, I'm going to just do a sharpshooter shot and see how this goes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Roll well. And I have inspiration, which I'm going to add to that. So that's-- That's 18 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 18 just hits. Scanlan comes through again!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you! That's very useful. Where's my other d12? Why do I never have any d12s? There they are.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, you're on deck, so be prepared.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Nine, ten, 11, 12-- That's 28 points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 28 points of damage!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And reload, and that's the end of my turn.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Kash, at the beginning of your turn, I need you to roll a dexterity save.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, good. I'm glad that we're all right next to a wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Vax, it's your turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can you show me where his glance is, again, with that doohickus you have?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have to guess. You don't know the specific elements of his ray.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to use Cunning Action to hide.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, first and foremost, at the beginning of your turn, I need you to make a dexterity save.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, evasion. Yeah, don't worry. That's 30.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Don't say you're using evasion yet. It doesn't damage you. Or you could have wasted your evasion for no reason. However, you do avoid being grappled as they try and grab onto you and hold onto you. You can use your bonus action to--?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hide.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, roll a stealth check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is a 25.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 25, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to run past Trinket along the back wall, all around the edge, over to where Vex and Kash are.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to keep pushing along--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This is our spot!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to get between them, and I know that slows the movement down, but I want to be in there, and I'm going to use my two actions left to throw daggers at the floating fucking bowling ball with eyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I duck so he can shoot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do not have advantage, because it rolled a natural 20 on its perception check and you are not stealthed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, wow. So the first one is a 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Second one is a 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That misses.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So it's a measly little attack of nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nine points of damage. A little toothpick impact. That ends your turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No! I use the last of my movement to lower down behind Kash. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Wow.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, so you place the residuum across where the diamond dust was shattered and use that as almost a focus for the energy from the spell. All right, I need you to go ahead and roll an intelligence check. Just roll a d20 and add your intelligence modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Call whose attention forth?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What are you hesitating for? Do it. Whatever the fuck you're going to do, do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: She could destroy everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do you mean, she could destroy? What do you mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Who's she?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I mean the dragons are going to be the least of your fears if she comes back. That's what I mean.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Kashaw, you're running out of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Who's she?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to bring her back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. So, we're going to do a quick ritual skill challenge as part of this revivify enchantment. So as you grasp the symbol, and as you do, you can see all of the scars across your forearm begin to light with a deep red crimson energy, just (hissing sound), and you close your eyes, and as you look up, you can see Kash's eyes just go straight black, and this dark energy begins to swirl off, and as it reaches out, it touches the diamonds, which then shatter upon impact, and the dark energy seeps into your sister's corpse. The back arches, though she does not register on the face or any of her movement. Her body lifts slightly in place, her arms just draped, dangling and scraping against the stone beneath her as she's elevated there. How do you guys want to help with this ritual?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I take out three of my shards of residuum, and I place them in where the crystals are as an extra offering.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, so as you take the moonstone and hold it aloft, you place it near the focus of Kashaw's ritual. The dull white glow begins to intermingle with the dark, shattered energy of Vesh's grant, and as they impact, it creates this strange grey swirl. I need you to go ahead and roll an arcana check. Roll a d20 and add your arcana skill to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I take out three of my shards of residuum, and I place them in where the crystals are as an extra offering.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you place the residuum across where the diamond dust was shattered and use that as almost a focus for the energy from the spell. All right, I need you to go ahead and roll an intelligence check. Just roll a d20 and add your intelligence modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to throw up. Oh no.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I can use my rerolls as well, correct? I can't use my--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's for saving throws, not for ability checks. What did you roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Six.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Six. The energy itself vibrates within the glass shards, which then shatter, seemingly not able to focus the energy of the spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I take my enchanted moonstone from my staff and place it next to her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so as you take the moonstone, and-- are you speaking to your patron to aid with this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I am.
